description: Return to the LM and Closeout

tokens: Okay, let's head for Blocky Crater.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Al, do you have a sample bag number on that last one?
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: All those rocks are too big for sample bags...
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: They're all at least 6 inches in diameter, and I think these are some of the ones you wanted.
It's kind of hard to tell without having a photograph on hand or something and standing there and studying it for a lot longer than I think we care to do it.
(That is, it's hard to tell) just which rocks are which.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: It's pretty easy to move along on this slope.
It's just a little bit deeper and it's a little bit softer.
I'm going to take a break here, Pete, for just a few seconds.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Can't get up your pace when you're running on the side of a slope.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: You don't have a chance to go from side to side, like on level ground.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Look at that huge boulder out there at...Boy, I wish we could go over there.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Over the top of the hill.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Don't see where you are looking.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Right on the other side of the...About 200 yards that way.
See that big boulder sitting up there; the biggest one we've seen since we've been here.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I don't see which one you're referring to.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Oh, these down in here are bigger than that!
Look at that right there on your left!
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Let me turn around and look.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: That's a pretty good sized one.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Let's get up out of the crater where we can get up on the level ground.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: It was kind of hung up on the gnomon.
Let me get the gnomon and pick it up.
It's not usually on there and...(Pause; breathing fairly heavily) (Garbled) gnomon.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Okay, let's document up a sample here, and I think you ought to photo (that is, do a partial pan of) that whole Blocky Crater right there.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: That's got to be bedrock there, babe.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Let's get some samples of that.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Hey, Houston, (garbled) the dimple crater's right behind the LM.
It's a big, blocky impact crater.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I kind of thought it would be tough down in the crater, losing your balance, but it doesn't seem to be; it's just harder walking, that's all.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Pete and Al, could we have EMU check?
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: We're right at the top of the rim, we can get a good place to rest.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: We're going to sample...I'll tell you what we're going to do, Houston.

We're going to get an EMU check here; we're going to pick up one sample out of this blocky crater; give you a partial pan of it because it's a pretty fantastically interesting crater with a lot of bedrock.
Big chunky rocks blown up out of it...
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: ...and get a sample of the double craters on the side of the Surveyor crater, and then my recommendation is (that) we've got so much gear and so many rocks, that we head for the LM and start packing it all up.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: We concur; that's a good idea.
Al, could you give us your (oxygen) percent?
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: And how are you doing on that film?
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Boy, my camera is completely dust-covered, Houston.
I just hope that the lens is open...
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Your lens is in good shape.
Now, why don't you stand right here and get a partial pan - while you're resting - on this crater?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Pause) I'm beginning to think that these rocks (garbled), if we'd just crack them open, we'd find they're plain old basalt rock on the inside.
We just don't ever have any cracked.
We ought to pound one of those things with a
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Okay, you want me to do a pan of this little part (that is, Block Crater) or the whole (Surveyor) crater, Pete?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Get about four shots across it and then move over and get another four (that is, to get stereo).
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Funny, I can notice that TV camera on my back.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Does it feel okay back there?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Sure, the way to carry gear; it sure beats that thing (the Hand Tool Carrier).
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Noticing that Al has started a pan of the interior of Surveyor Crater) Now, wait a minute; where are you shooting, Al?
I want you to shoot down in that (Block) crater right there.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Sorry, that's what I...Okay, read you.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Shoot way down into it; get a stereo of that thing with those big blocks down there.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: It's kind of dark (meaning that the interior of Blocky Crater is in shadow), but I think we can get something good.
This is probably the most spectacular crater we've come to, I think.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: The original crater (meaning Surveyor Crater) took it down to bedrock and then, I guess, more recently then, this one (meaning Block Crater) came in here and really banged it out.
These blocks are a lot more sharp-cornered than any we've seen anywhere else.
I guess this must be the most recent one we've been around.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Now, I got the idea that the bedrock's not too deep (that is, the bedrock is not covered by much regolith), and that this (Surveyor Crater) was a big crater but it's very, very, very, very old.
And then this thing came along and hit it...
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: ...and broke into the side of the bedrock that's been sticking out into this...
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Yeah, and then threw it all out again.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: ...original crater, and threw it all out again.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Let's get a sample of that rock.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I think it's going to be the same (basalts that they've been sampling all day)...
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: And then let's get out of here.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: We document (that is, take a "before" picture) and (collect) a couple of the big pieces.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: What looks like all the same?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Let me get a shot at it, Pete, cross-Sun.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: We don't need the gnomon; I'll put the...
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Say, by the way, when I shot that crater down there, I had my distance set on 30 feet.
I thought that would be right, but that's the only one we haven't shot on the numbers.
(Pause) (Talking quietly to himself) Oh, man; too far back, Bean.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: This is going to be sample bag number - (looking) number 15D, Houston.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Pete's going to put two or three rocks in here, just generally (selected); and I'll photograph them (that is, get some "afters") and we can see what he took.
You know, most of the rocks we've seen today is exactly like this.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Going to pound one of these with a hammer in a minute.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Hey, there's some of that light-colored undersoil.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: You want me to get another sample bag?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I want to start moving out.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I'll just pick up this one big rock here, Pete, and stick it in the bag.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Now, I'm going to go pack up the doc(umented) samples box.
(Pause) And I understand you're going to allow me 20 pounds of other rocks (that is, rocks he can't get into the rock box).
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Pete, what we'd like to do is to get an estimate from you of how much you think you've got in the first SRC (Sample Return Container, the rock box) in terms of volume or weight.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Well, in comparison to the zero-g (means one-sixth g) airplane let's see, the maximum load is 80 total pounds, right?
I'm going to guess that the box that I sent up (after EVA-1) was about a 60-pounder.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: From what you said in the first EVA and basic calculations on Apollo 11 data, we come up with about 54 pounds.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: I think that we're fairly close.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I'll just bet you (that) everything we got here is really black basalt.
All been colored just like that Surveyor.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Hey, that (Surveyor parts) bag is bouncing a little bit too much back there, Pete.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Al, you've got to get that close-up stereo camera (Gold Camera) going.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: CDR (Commander) is back at the LM.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: You're 3 plus 16 into the EVA, and for a 4-hour EVA, you're right on.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Hey, did you know that our EVA antenna didn't go straight up?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I noticed that when I was running back.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: How far off the nominal position was it?
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: This thing is driving me buggy.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Hey, I think all this stuff is just fine-grain basalt, Pete.
We haven't seen anything else but that.
We haven't seen anything at any of the places that we've gone except the same type of fine-grained basalt.
It's been different colors because of how long it's been out on the surface or where it's been.
It'll be interesting when we get them to Houston and they crack them open.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Take a rest here a moment.
(Pause) I used to have to push the legs down in that lunar Hand Tool Carrier, but I don't have to anymore.
It just pushes its own legs; got enough rocks in there.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Hey, here's where that engine moved some dirt.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Where are you on that, Al?
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: I'm right to the left rear of the...It looks like I'm between the plus Y and minus Z strut, (that is, northeast of the LM) and it looks like it really washed a lot of dirt off in this direction.
If I look back behind me,...
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: We'(ve) got to get out of here (that is, get back in the spacecraft and get ready for launch at 142:03).
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I want you to put the tool carrier right here, (next to the MESA so that he can get the bag out of the HTC and empty it into the rock box).
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Stick your camera (the CDR Hasselblad, which Al has mounted on his RCU with Mag 48 attached) in the ETB and get the film can (meaning Mag 49) out of there (meaning the HTC bag), and I'm going to start packing up the gear.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I'm not sure...I guess all this will fit in here (in the ETB); our cameras and everything, complete with this (LM) TV camera.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Pause) Don't lift it up, Al, or my rock box will go.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Do you want to take my saddlebag off?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Pause; as he gets the hammer off the HTC) Got it?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Pause) (Subvocal) All right, maybe I've got it.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I'll tell you what, you go get me the solar wind first.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I'll go get it for you.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: You want me...Let me take that little bag out there with me.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: You're all tangled up in the TV cable.
I'll tell you, that TV cable's gonna be...(It's) really making me mad.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Let's take the Surveyor TV camera off.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Lay it at the (west) footpad (below the ladder).
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: What do you want to do with it?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I want you to put it in the (LM) footpad.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Let me set this down, then I'll go get it.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I'll tell you, it's a trap.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Pause, as he loosens the parts bag straps) (I've got to) get this other bottom one, Pete.
(Long Pause) Kind of turn this way, would you (to make the parts bag more accessible)?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (The parts bag is) so much lighter (than the rockboxes or ETB).
We're not going to have any trouble running this one up the LEC.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Well, this LEC...Oh boy, I made a mistake.
I should have brought the tool cutter back with me (to cut the LM TV cable so that he could get it out of the way by tossing it under the LM).
This (LM) TV cable...Just put it (the Surveyor camera) in the footpad (probably at the base of the ladder).
This TV cable is going to drive me crazy.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Want me to take it and move it out of the area?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I just tried to throw it under the LM now...
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I can grab one end and just pull it out if you want.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: It's all tangled up in the LEC, now.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Okay, I'll put this...Where do you want me to put this?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I'm going to straighten this LEC out right now.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Now, look, when I disappear with the LEC (that is, when he gets it untangled and out of the way), you get that TV cable and get it out of here.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Pause as he tears the cable loose from the LM)
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Now, just throw the TV cable under the spacecraft.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Okay, I want you to get the solar wind.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Let me get this TV cable out of the way.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Pete, would you hold this just one second, and then we'll get rid of this cable forever?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Where do you want me to go with it?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Then I can get this out over here, see.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: There; now you've only got it one place...
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Leave it there and I'll get the solar wind.
This the bag...This the bag you want me to use (for the solar wind), Pete?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Solar wind doesn't like to roll up much.
(Pause) Little rascal, doesn't want to roll up.
(I'll) just wrap it around here best I can, without getting any dirt on it.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I got dust all over the rock box, and I'm trying to blow it off.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Houston, we got that solar wind, but it didn't roll up in a very neat package.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Hey, it sure didn't, did it?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: It split right near the top.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: You can hold that, and I'll just try to roll it up as best I can without getting any...I already got a little dirt on it that's not doing any good.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Not a lot I can do about it.
I'm sure it (the solar wind)'s a good experiment.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Here, let me hold this end, and you just wrap it tight.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: And chase down any of those noble gases or whatever that crud is.
(To Gibson) Looks bad, but I think it will do the job, Houston.
We squashed it in so it's...
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: It's right...Let me get it for you.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: It just doesn't look so good, Houston.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Give me a hand getting this rock box closed.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Hey, that's a nice full box.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Got something for you right there on the top of it.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Now, I've got to get this dirt off it (the rock box seal) somehow.
There's a brush and a scribe.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Why didn't I think of that earlier.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Then you can just take out the seal (means the spacer)...There you go.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Pete, that glass brush should be over there on the Hand Tool Carrier if that will be of use to you.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: The rock box is closed, Houston.
Al, I want all the big ones.
(Weighing a bag with rocks in it) That looks like about 1 inch to me.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Okay, you got some of those bedrock ones in there, (in the rock box) didn't you?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: What do you want me to do with that?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: We'd like to give you a little weight summary for the rock boxes.
If...We estimate you probably got about the same in rock box 2 as you did in rock box 1.
The Surveyor parts and TV camera we show a nominal 25 pounds and 15.
What you could put in your bag that goes on the floor is about 15 pounds worth of rocks; and in the left-hand side stowage bag, you can put about 25 pounds of rock.
So I guess those are the two you are working for now.
Fifteen pounds worth of rocks in the bag on the floor, and 25 pounds on the left-hand side stowage bag.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Okay, we don't have that many rocks, Houston.
I'll tell you what we've got.
We've got...SRC 2 is full and closed.
It's...Gosh, I hope I got it all in there, let me see: solar wind, core tubes, environmental gas sample, (and the) documented samples all made it in.
And the box is full, and I closed it, and I've got about...What's 1 inch (displacement) on my scale?
I've got about 1 inch worth of rocks in another bag.
And that's it; that's all the rocks we('ve) got.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Roger, stand by for that number, Pete.
(Pause) Pete, that l-inch displacement is about 10 to 15 pounds.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Okay, now, let me ask you another question.
I can get some more rocks.
While Al is taking stereophotos (with the Gold camera).
We'll see if I can get ourselves (some more rocks)...
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Okay, I'm going to...I don't have a (70 mm) camera to go along with this (for documentation), so I'll just tell Houston when I'm taking a picture and then they'll know.
So they can keep up with it.

On this stereo camera, I'm taking a picture now, about 10 feet from the LM between the plus Y (north) and minus Z (east) strut, and I am hoping to show the effects of the engine exhaust on the lunar surface.
Just moving around here to do it.
The little counter doesn't seem to be working.
Everything is working okay but the little counter.
And I am taking the fourth picture right up next to the engine, here.
(Pause) Okay, another one close to the engine.
The little counter on top of the experiment's not working, so I'll just tell you what I take next.
And the light and everything seems to be working, so I assume it's probably taking pictures.
I'm going to go look for a crater that is undisturbed and take a picture down inside it.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I'm in an area now, Houston.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Okay, we recommend that you pack up where you are and start trying to pack up the excess rocks you just got and think about ingress.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Houston, I'd like to comment to all the people who are involved with this EVA, my congratulations.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Well, I think you two folks did an excellent job.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: I'll take some of these pictures until you give me a call, Pete.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Why don't you just start working your way over here, Al?
And we've got an awful lot of gear, and we will start getting her up.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: We've got about 22 minutes (left in the EVA) by my clock.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: And we got a long day in front of us.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Oh, this is so much fun.
I can jump up about 3 feet and do a 180.
(Laughs) See if I can do a 360!
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: You got to watch it though.
You get all that mass going around and you get in trouble.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: What's up with Yankee Clipper this morning, Houston?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Pete, Yankee Clipper looks real good.
He's been doing P22's (that is, looking at the LM) and rolling right off.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Okay, Pete, I'll take this as my last one.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (To Gibson) If he can see the Surveyor, has he been able to see us out there?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I don't know how many we've got...
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I think we got all the film in (the ETB), didn't we, Pete?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Reading his checklist two lines below 2+55) "Clean EMU." Okay, clean me, and I'll get in there (in the cabin), and we'll start pulling this (Surveyor parts bag) in (on the
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Now, one thing I need to do is...Hand me the Surveyor...
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Damn things are getting jammed (with dust).
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Let's see, put it (Surveyor TV camera) back on the footpad.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Not supposed to get (the parts bag) too dirty.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Why don't you hop up on the ladder, and let me brush you off from the ladder?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Okay, why don't you brush me off up high here and then I'll hop up and you can get me.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Ah, everything's hard to get off.
Let me get up here (on the ladder).
Now, I'm going to kick my feet, so it's going to get this Surveyor bag dirty.
(Pause while Pete moves the parts bag)
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Jumping up the ladder and, possibly, missing the first rung) Ow!
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I think I'm making myself dirtier (by dusting Al).
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Is that it (that is, is Pete finished dusting Al)?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Long Pause as Al climbs the ladder)
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Okay, I'll watch you in (through) the hatch.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Just when you get the door open.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (I'd better) put up my golden visor.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: You might want to turn off your PLSS feedwater when you get all the way in.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Okay, just a second (while he gets in position to operate the LEC).
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I've got to get this sequence camera out of the window; I don't want to leave it in here when I'm in here.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Pause) Did you ever get the picture of the LM and Earth?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: It's up over the LM now.
It's the first time I've had a chance to look.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Let me mount the bag (he probably means the LEC).
Hey, you got to give me a little slack (on the LEC), Pete.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Just a second; I'll be okay.
Don't put any weight on it yet.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Surveyor (parts bag) we just pull in like this.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Okay, Houston, the Surveyor gear is up, coming back for SRC 2.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Take them...Wait a minute...Oh, no; not yet...Not yet.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: The name of the game is don't rush and do it right.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Oh, here I go get you the rock box.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: You may have it tangled on something, Pete.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Never mind, I see what the problem is.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Lift away, you've got a rock box.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Feel how heavy this thing is.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I'm going to have to use the pull-in feature; just a second.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Give her one big heave (to clear the porch?).
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: That darn sun is in (his eyes)...
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Okay, take up some tension and I'll pull it in.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: And am I filthy dirty from that LEC!
(Long Pause while Al gets the rock box off the LEC)
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Long Pause while they get the attachment hardware back out to Pete)
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Pause) Really...Getting in and out of these shadows really gets eerie looking.
Okay, Al, if you'll wait just a minute.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Passing over the thing (the porch lip)?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Okay, pull up a little bit and I'll bring it in.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: The Sun's shining right in my eyes.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Beautiful job; just throw the LEC out.
ETB is in with the (LM TV) camera and all the film, and so forth and so on.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Copy you got the ETB in with the TV camera, close-up stereo (film magazine), and the (70 mm) film packs.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Okay, now, Pete, here comes the LEC, so watch out.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Man, that really comes out, doesn't it?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Pete, you didn't "roger" the film pack.
Do you have all the film packs and the close-up stereo film, as well as the (LM) TV in that ETB?
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Close-up stereo film was in the ETB, two black-and-white magazines and one camera.
I threw the other (70 mm) camera away because it was broken.
And the TV camera went up, so I believe we've got everything, Ed.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: You got the...Also the one camera which had the third film pack on?
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: The third film pack never got used.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Okay, we got three film packs and one camera up there right now.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Did you send the film back up?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Okay, we sent all three film packs back up.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: There is something I wanted to...
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: That's my low water pressure, Houston.
I just turned off my water.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Pete, also how about the tools?
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: You might want to turn off your water too, Pete.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Yeah, I knew...I was sitting here thinking there was something I ought to do.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Houston, I guess you can mark me off the lunar surface; I'm on the footpad.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: We got that, Pete, at 3 hours and 50 minutes into the EVA.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Okay, up-a (sic) the ladder I come.
Boy, I'll tell you, Al, that LEC really got me dirty.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Yeah, it flips that dirt all around.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Oh, wait a minute; I know what I want to do.
(Getting the map, see comments below) There we go.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: You want to give me that piece of paper?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Okay, Houston, that's my PLSS feedwater (tone because the supply has been turned off), no sweat.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Come to your left a little bit.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Got to kind of scoot a little bit further in.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: And I wonder what that's from.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: That little...Wait, you need to kick out that piece...
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I think that came from off of the landing gear.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Joking about how dirty Pete has gotten) You are nice and clean.
(Dick) Gordon will be glad to see you.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Okay, let me move out of the way.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Tell me when (to start the repressurization).
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Pause as he turns to change the lighting)
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: PLSS O2 Off when you get 3.6 (psi).
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Let me see if I can.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Okay, going to be taking mine (his PLSS O2) off.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: What's the rest of the checklist?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I hope that (hatch) seal is good.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I didn't check that seal real closely (for dust).
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: It says here: "Verify cabin pressure increasing." (Garbled) "PLSS O2, Off.
Activate OPS purge valve to depress suit as required." (Pause)
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Pause) Hey, don't take your helmet off!
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Yeah, we do, because we have to put on our other ones.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Okay, let me read to you.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Verify cabin pressure stable at 4.6." Looks good to me, Pete.
"Post-EVA systems Config(uration): Verify EV circ..." Look over there and make sure Suit Fan 1 (circuit breaker) is closed.
And I'll make sure that Suit Fan Delta-P (breaker) is closed.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Pause as he turns) Suit Fan 1 is closed.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: We're going to get some ECS caution and
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Our ECS Caution and H2O Sep(arator) component lights will go out in a minute.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: "Descent H2O valve open." Okay, that (getting a drink of water)'s a good idea, boy.
